input:
He then explained that he came to Madinah only because it was time for a new Prophet to appear and that Madinah would be the place to which he would emigrate. “I hoped”, he said, “that he would appear before I died so that I could follow him. His appearance is very imminent, so let no one follow him ahead of you. He will be given power to shed blood and take the women and children of his opponents prisoner, but that should not deter you from following him, fellow Jews.” When the Prophet emigrated to Madinah and had his battle with the Jews of Qurayẓah (which will be described later) a few young men from that tribe reminded their people that Muhammad was the Prophet that Ibn al-Hayyabān had told them about.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 4: The Makkan Scene at the Start of Prophethood

output.scene_title: The Prophet's Message to the Jews
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 4: The Makkan Scene at the Start of Prophethood

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a green cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by green cloak and subtle warm light.

characters.name: Ibn al-Hayyabān
characters.appearance: Mid 40s, dark olive skin, athletic build, rugged features. Thinning brown hair, trimmed beard. Wearing a worn-out brown woolen cloak, beige thobe, and a white cloth wrapped as a head covering. Worn leather sandals. Expressive eyes, determined look.

characters.name: Jewish Leader from Qurayẓah
characters.appearance: Late 30s, medium build, stern features. Dark hair, trimmed beard. Wearing a simple beige thobe, brown woolen cloak, and a white cloth wrapped as a head covering. Worn leather sandals. Frowning eyes, defensive posture.

output.moral_lesson: The path of truth and righteousness may be difficult, but it is always worth following, even in the face of adversity.
output.narrative_text: The Prophet told the Jews that a new Prophet would soon appear in Madinah and that they should not follow him ahead of time. However, when the Prophet emigrated to Madinah, some young Jews from Qurayẓah remembered a man named Ibn al-Hayyabān who had told them about the Prophet's coming.

compliance.notes: Prophet shown from behind, backlit by sunlight. Ibn al-Hayyabān and the Jewish leader shown with full faces, engaging in conversation. Neutral scene, suitable for children.

output.image_prompt: Traditional Arabic illustration. Exterior of a simple 7th century Makkah mud-brick home. A man in white thobe and green cloak, white turban, seen entirely from behind, standing in the doorway with warm sunlight behind him. In the background, two men: a rugged-looking older Arab man in brown cloak and beige thobe, and a younger Arab man with stern features in simple beige thobe and brown cloak. Both men are shown in the shade, looking at each other in conversation. Simple mud-brick walls, clay water jug in corner. Medium shot, eye level. Earthy color palette. No modern objects.

metadata.chapter_title: The Makkan Scene at the Start of Prophethood
